objective: Close GAP-006 by aligning the policy-gate risk chain with the stated safety floor: high-risk changes must fail closed under the governed contract/defaults instead of passing as warn-only, while medium-risk advisory behavior remains explicit and tested.
feedbackSignal: The adopted evidence-led audit identifies a contradiction between the contract's human-mediated safety floor for high-risk changes and policy-gate chains that can classify high-risk files as warn/pass.
rootOperationalFailure: High-risk policy behavior is partly encoded as prose in north-star/autonomy language while the executable policyChain can still pass high-risk work through a warning action.
durableSystemImprovement: Make the contract/default policy chain and policy-gate tests fail closed for high-risk files so the executable gate matches the stated safety boundary.

inScope: Change the governed repository contract policyChain so high maps to block and block maps to fail.
Change the default contract policyChain so newly scaffolded or omitted-policyChain contracts inherit high-risk fail-closed behavior.
Update focused policy-gate tests so high-risk files fail when no max-tier is supplied and when max-tier is high.
Preserve medium-risk warn/pass behavior when the contract explicitly allows medium warnings.
Add or update focused tests that prove low-risk and empty-file cases still pass.
Update security/governance documentation only where needed to state executable policy-gate behavior without broadening the slice.
outOfScope: Do not introduce decision-request/v1, approval ledgers, or tracked exception schemas in this slice; those belong to GAP-005 or a later exception-management slice.
Do not change PR closeout, delivery-truth, runtime-card, review-state, external-state, or harness next behavior.
Do not add network-backed human approval checks or mutate GitHub/Linear state.
Do not make medium-risk files fail unless the existing contract explicitly configures medium as block/fail.
Do not claim merge readiness or external-state freshness from local policy-gate validation.

assumptions: The narrowest safe implementation is contract/default policy-chain alignment plus policy-gate regression coverage, not a new approval system.

Existing contract validation intentionally permits warn/block/allow combinations, so this slice changes governed defaults and repository contract values rather than banning all warn-on-high configurations globally.

Medium-risk warn/pass is still acceptable because the audit calls out high-risk action approval drift and the north-star text permits low and medium-risk automation when deterministic evidence and rollback are clear.
A future tracked-exception slice can reintroduce high-risk pass-through only when an explicit owner-approved exception receipt exists.
acceptanceCriteria: harness.contract.json maps policyChain.tierToAction.high to block and policyChain.actionToVerdict.block to fail.
DEFAULT_POLICY_CHAIN maps high to block while preserving medium warn and low allow.
The omitted-policyChain fallback path is explicitly covered: a minimal contract such as test-fixtures/contract.json must inherit DEFAULT_POLICY_CHAIN and fail closed for high-risk files.
A high-risk path such as src/auth/login.ts produces passed=false, action=block, verdict=fail with no max-tier.
A high-risk path such as src/auth/login.ts produces passed=false, action=block, verdict=fail even when max-tier=high.
A high-risk path with max-tier=medium continues to fail with maxAllowed=medium and the violating file listed.
A medium-risk path such as src/lib/utils.ts continues to pass with action=warn and verdict=pass when max-tier=medium.
A low-risk test path and an empty file list continue to pass.
CLI JSON behavior returns exit code 1 for the governed high-risk case and exit code 0 for medium/low pass cases.
CLI JSON for policy-chain-blocked high-risk files distinguishes policy action blocking from max-tier threshold violations instead of reporting an unset maximum as if it were a configured threshold.
Max-tier violations fail closed with verdict=fail even if a custom contract attempts to remap policyChain.actionToVerdict.block to pass.
Contract validation and JSON schema reject actionToVerdict.block values other than fail.

This slice introduces no tracked-exception schema. High-risk pass-through remains unsupported; any future exception path must be linked to GAP-005 or a dedicated exception-management slice and must not be implied by warn/pass behavior.
Documentation does not imply high-risk changes are human-mediated while the executable contract still lets them pass as warn-only.
Goal-board validation still passes after the slice receipt is recorded.
validationGates: node --import tsx src/cli.ts policy-gate --contract harness.contract.json --files src/auth/login.ts --json
node --import tsx src/cli.ts policy-gate --contract harness.contract.json --files src/lib/utils.ts --max-tier medium --json

node_modules/.bin/biome check harness.contract.json src/lib/contract/types-core.ts src/lib/contract/validator-core.ts src/lib/contract/json-schema-core.ts src/lib/contract/validator.test.ts src/commands/contract.test.ts src/commands/policy-gate.ts src/commands/policy-gate.test.ts src/lib/output/normalise-policy-gate.ts test-fixtures/contract-block-pass.json docs/agents/06-security-and-governance.md .harness/intent/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit-pu-020-gap-006-policy-gate-risk-chain-intent.json

git diff --check -- harness.contract.json src/lib/contract/types-core.ts src/lib/contract/validator-core.ts src/lib/contract/json-schema-core.ts src/lib/contract/validator.test.ts src/commands/contract.test.ts src/commands/policy-gate.ts src/commands/policy-gate.test.ts src/lib/output/normalise-policy-gate.ts test-fixtures/contract-block-pass.json docs/agents/06-security-and-governance.md .harness/implementation-notes/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit.mdx .harness/implementation-notes/implementation-notes.html docs/goals/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit/state.yaml docs/goals/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit/receipts.jsonl .harness/intent/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit-pu-020-gap-006-policy-gate-risk-chain-intent.json
rollback: Restore harness.contract.json policyChain.high to warn only if an explicit owner decision accepts high-risk warn-only behavior.
Restore DEFAULT_POLICY_CHAIN.high to warn only with a matching spec/goal exception and regression tests showing the accepted exception path.
Revert focused policy-gate expectations if intent review proves policy-gate is intentionally advisory and the safety floor belongs to a separate gate.
stopConditions: Intent review finds an existing tracked-exception or approval mechanism that must be wired before changing high-risk behavior.
Focused tests show downstream scaffold compatibility depends on high-risk warn/pass and cannot be safely updated in this slice.
Contract validation fails in a way that requires changing public contract schema semantics rather than repository defaults.
